prior.content_md:
This document is a Regulatory Fact Document covering Regulation (EU) 2023/1804 on the deployment of alternative fuels infrastructure (AFIR), published in the EU Official Journal on 22 September 2023 and applicable from 13 April 2024. It supersedes Directive 2014/94/EU (AFID).

The document is anchored to operations in which road transport is the secondary mode (behind air-primary), serving European trade lanes across cargo verticals including live events, fine art, luxury goods, film and TV, high-value automotive, and humanitarian. The workspace operates as a freight forwarder.

AFIR imposes no direct compliance obligations on freight forwarders as a regulated entity class. undefined undefined However, AFIR's infrastructure deployment mandates directly govern the operational viability of the zero-emission and alternative-fuel road vehicles that the workspace's road carriers will be required to operate under parallel EU regulations (specifically the amended Heavy-Duty Vehicle CO₂ standards). undefined undefined The workspace must understand this regulation to evaluate carrier capability, manage client commitments on sustainable transport, and anticipate road-mode cost exposure on European lanes.

**Convention notes:** All deadlines are expressed in calendar dates. "TEN-T core network" and "TEN-T comprehensive network" are the trans-European transport network tiers defined under EU infrastructure policy. "Urban nodes" are cities and agglomerations defined within the TEN-T framework. Regulatory text citations are to the Official Journal version of the regulation.

*Source: Regulation (EU) 2023/1804, EUR-Lex/Official Journal of the European Union, 22 September 2023. https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2023/1804/oj/eng

---
